scenario: Delta Insurance writes a Camunda 8.8 process test for an invoice approval flow with 3 worker classes including an ArchiveInvoiceWorker that calls a legacy ArchiveService. The team wants the test to cover the BPMN model and its glue code without contacting the real archive system on every CI run.
question: Which scope should the Delta team include in the process test for the invoice approval flow?

options.text: Cover the BPMN model plus glue code such as worker classes, FEEL expressions, and any client API that maps data before calling the Camunda Client, while mocking workflow engine-independent business services
Cover the BPMN model only, exercising gateway FEEL expressions but excluding all worker classes and client API code; glue code belongs to a separate worker-unit scope
Cover the BPMN model and every backend system the workers call, including the real archive system, so the process test detects regressions across the full production landscape on every run
Cover the workers and FEEL expressions in isolation, without deploying the BPMN model, and rely on integration tests to verify that the model and the glue code work together

a.text: Correct. The Test scope and mocking section says the process test should cover the executable BPMN definition plus glue code that logically belongs to it: workers, FEEL expressions for gateways and IO mappings, and any client API doing data mapping before the Camunda Client call. Engine-independent business services are mocked.

b.text: Incorrect. The best practice puts workers, FEEL, and the client API inside the process test scope; only engine-independent business services like ArchiveService are mocked out.

c.text: Incorrect. The process test deliberately mocks engine-independent business services such as the real ArchiveService to stay fast and isolated; full backend coverage belongs to integration tests.

d.text: Incorrect. The process test must include the BPMN model itself; isolating workers and FEEL alone does not validate the data flowing through the process.

explanation: A Camunda 8.8 process test covers the executable BPMN definition together with the glue code that belongs to it, including workers, FEEL expressions, and client-side mapping APIs, while mocking workflow engine-independent business services.
